records.ety: [OF. relinquir, L. relinquere to leave behind; pref. re- re + linquere to leave. See Loan, and cf. Relic, Relict.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Relinquished (-kw?sht); p. pr. & vb. n. Relinquishing.]

defs: To withdraw from; to leave behind; to desist from; to abandon; to quit; as, to relinquish a pursuit.
quotes: We ought to relinquish such rites.
They placed Irish tenants upon the lands relinquished by the English.

defs: To give up; to renounce a claim to; resign; as, to relinquish a debt.
